discovery.run_summary: Semiconductor equipment names are being hammered (VECO -18%, ONTO -12%, AEIS -13%) alongside a broader SMH -4.5% breakdown, while defensive sectors (XLV, XLP, XLU) and financials rally — a classic rotation day. Defense continues its strong run with AVAV extending post-earnings gains. No Trump posts today; catalyst set is thin outside the semi-cap-equipment complex.

notable_index_moves: SMH -4.54% while SPY only -0.13% — sharp semi-specific selloff
TSLA -7.49%, META -4.9% — mega-cap tech leadership breaking
XLF +1.53% despite KRE -1.52% — big banks up, regionals down (curve/credit divergence)

discoveries.name: Veeco Instruments Inc.
discoveries.setup: Semi-cap equipment sympathy selloff with no company-specific 8-K
discoveries.thesis: VECO is down sharply with no filed catalyst while peers ONTO and AEIS also crater on the same day — this looks like a sector-wide semi-cap capex re-rating (possibly triggered by a peer's guidance or a memory/foundry capex cut headline not in this feed). With SMH -4.5% and three cap-equipment names dropping double digits together, the move likely has legs beyond today because capex cycles reprice over weeks, not hours. VECO is the smallest and most levered to niche end-markets, so it tends to overshoot but continues drifting until a specific bounce catalyst appears.
discoveries.what_confirms: A peer (LRCX, AMAT, KLAC) guiding down WFE spend, or a memory customer capex cut announcement in the next 1-2 sessions; continued SMH weakness with cap-equipment underperforming semis broadly.
discoveries.what_kills: VECO issues a reassuring 8-K or pre-announcement; the sector move reverses on a benign explanation (e.g., single-analyst downgrade already fully absorbed); SMH bounces sharply Monday.
discoveries.what_to_learn: When 3+ names in the same narrow sub-industry (semi-cap equipment) drop double-digits on the same day with empty 8-K signals, the driver is almost always a peer catalyst or macro capex read — worth searching for the source rather than assuming coincidence.
discoveries.catalyst: Sector-wide semi-cap equipment selloff; no company-specific filing
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals for VECO, ONTO, AEIS while all three fall 12-18% same session — pattern implies external sector catalyst.
research_pointers: Search for a peer guide-down (LRCX, AMAT, KLAC, ASML) or memory/HBM capex cut news in the last 48 hours
Check VECO's exposure to memory/HBM vs logic customers — HBM slowdown narratives would hit it harder
Look at options flow / short interest changes on VECO
Compare to the last time VECO fell >15% in a session absent own-company news — how long did the drift continue?

discoveries.name: Onto Innovation Inc.
discoveries.setup: Semi-cap metrology name selling off with peers, no own 8-K
discoveries.thesis: Same sector catalyst chain as VECO/AEIS. ONTO is the higher-quality metrology name in the group; if the driver is a memory or HBM capex reset, ONTO's HBM/advanced-packaging exposure makes it directly in the crosshairs. Move likely continues until the specific catalyst is identified and digested.
discoveries.what_confirms: HBM capex cut confirmed from a hyperscaler or memory maker; SMH continues lower Monday; sell-side downgrades of ONTO/advanced packaging plays.
discoveries.what_kills: Catalyst turns out to be a one-off peer guide with no HBM read-through; ONTO issues reassuring commentary; SMH reverses.
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals; correlated -12% move with VECO/AEIS on a day SMH is -4.5%.
research_pointers: Identify the specific catalyst — likely a peer or hyperscaler headline
Assess ONTO's HBM/advanced packaging revenue mix vs mainstream logic
Compare valuation post-drop to prior cyclical troughs

discoveries.name: AeroVironment, Inc.
discoveries.setup: Post-earnings continuation in a strong defense tape
discoveries.thesis: AVAV filed an earnings 8-K on 6/29 and has extended sharply higher over five sessions, with today's move confirming momentum. ITA +1.8% and KTOS also up — defense tape is bid and drone/loitering munitions names are the leaders. The magnitude of the multi-day run is large but supported by both a real earnings catalyst and a persistent narrative (Ukraine/defense drone demand). No mispricing edge either way at this point — the move is doing what a good earnings beat in a hot subsector should do.
discoveries.what_confirms: Continued defense sector strength; further contract announcements; sell-side upward revisions.
discoveries.what_kills: A geopolitical de-escalation headline; a defense budget cut signal; the earnings quality on inspection turns out to be one-time (backlog pull-forward).
discoveries.catalyst: Q earnings 8-K filed 6/29 with 2.02 + 7.01 items; multi-day drone-defense momentum
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: 8-K items 2.02 (results) + 7.01 (FD disclosure) filed 6/29; stock has continued higher since, consistent with a beat-and-raise print.
research_pointers: Read the 8-K exhibit for actual guidance language and backlog figures
Check whether the 7.01 disclosure contained a contract or a conference presentation
Assess how much of the 5-day rally is driven by KTOS/peer sympathy vs AVAV specifics

discoveries.name: Lattice Semiconductor
discoveries.setup: Semi selloff amplified by a 1.01/2.03 debt-related 8-K
discoveries.thesis: LSCC filed an 8-K on 6/30 with items 1.01 (material agreement) + 2.03 (new financial obligation) — likely a debt raise or credit facility. Combined with the SMH -4.5% tape, the stock is down mid-single digits. A refinancing/new debt facility is usually not a material valuation event for a company of LSCC's size, so a portion of today's drop is likely sector-driven overshoot rather than filing-driven. Low confidence because the 1.01 could also be an acquisition financing (bearish if pricey) — the exhibit needs reading.
discoveries.what_confirms: The 8-K exhibit reveals a routine refinancing or benign credit facility; LSCC bounces disproportionately if SMH stabilizes.
discoveries.what_kills: The 1.01 turns out to be an expensive acquisition, dilutive convert, or covenant-loosening amendment that signals stress; semi weakness broadens.
discoveries.what_to_learn: 1.01 + 2.03 in combination is ambiguous — needs the exhibit read; treating it as auto-bearish or auto-benign both lose money.
discoveries.catalyst: New material agreement + financial obligation 8-K on 6/30, plus sector selloff
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Items 1.01 + 2.03 in same filing typically indicate a financing transaction; magnitude of stock move is closer to sector beta than to a specific negative surprise.
research_pointers: Read the LSCC 8-K exhibit to identify whether 1.01 is financing, acquisition, or contract
Compare LSCC's decline to broader SMH beta on the day to isolate filing-specific impact
Check for any concurrent analyst commentary

discoveries.name: National Beverage Corp.
discoveries.setup: Post-earnings pop on a small-float consumer defensive
discoveries.thesis: Earnings 8-K filed 7/2 (items 2.02 + 8.01) two days after the print, stock +7.5% on 2.6x volume — normal post-earnings response for a low-float, low-coverage name. Move looks proportional; no obvious mispricing.
discoveries.what_confirms: Follow-through in staples names on defensive rotation days; sell-side note upgrade.
discoveries.what_kills: Reversal on discovery that the 8.01 item contained a negative disclosure (guidance withdrawal, litigation).
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: 2.02 earnings item plus 8.01 filed 7/2; recent_earnings confirms report 2 days ago; magnitude and volume consistent with a modest beat.
research_pointers: Confirm the 8.01 content is benign
Check whether insider Nick Caporella issued any commentary — FIZZ is famously opaque

discoveries.name: Kratos Defense
discoveries.setup: Defense/drone sympathy with AVAV in a bid ITA tape
discoveries.thesis: KTOS is riding the same drone/defense narrative as AVAV, with no own 8-K. Five-day return ~20% suggests a lot has already been priced in, but with ITA +1.8% and defense as a Trump-friendly sector, momentum can persist. No clear mispricing to trade against.
discoveries.what_confirms: Further contract wins; continued ITA strength.
discoveries.what_kills: Geopolitical thaw; a KTOS-specific negative headline; profit-taking after such a strong run.
discoveries.catalyst: Sympathy with AVAV earnings + broad defense strength
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals; move correlated with AVAV and ITA.
research_pointers: Track relative strength of KTOS vs AVAV — if it starts underperforming, sympathy trade is exhausted
Check for any pending Pentagon contract awards

discoveries.name: CRISPR Therapeutics

discoveries.thesis: CRSP +8% with XBI +2.5% is roughly 3x sector beta, suggesting some company-specific catalyst exists but is not in the SEC feed (could be conference presentation, analyst note, or Casgevy commercial data leak). Without identifying the driver, I cannot commit to overdone or underdone at conf 3+.
discoveries.what_confirms: n/a — need to identify catalyst first
discoveries.what_kills: n/a — need to identify catalyst first
discoveries.catalyst: Unknown — sector strength plus unidentified company-specific driver
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals; magnitude exceeds XBI beta but no filing.
research_pointers: Search for CRSP-specific news: Casgevy sales updates, partnership news, analyst upgrades, conference presentations
Check VRTX (partner) for correlated moves
Options flow for gamma squeeze / positioning

catalyst_chains.primary_event: Sector-wide semi-cap equipment selloff (unidentified peer/customer capex catalyst)
AVAV strong earnings 8-K + drone defense narrative

ai_announcements.headline: Zuckerberg tells staff AI agents haven't progressed as quickly as hoped
ai_announcements.summary: Meta CEO reportedly admitted internally that AI agent development is behind schedule. This is a notable sentiment marker - a top-3 AI spender expressing disappointment could ripple through AI capex-linked names, though it could also imply Meta needs MORE compute/effort, not less.

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Veeco sells semi cap equipment (laser annealing, ion beam, MOCVD) used in advanced logic/HBM production. Zuck's comment doesn't structurally reduce AI capex - hyperscalers still building. A -18% single-day move on a headline this soft is disproportionate; more likely company-specific (guide-down, lost socket, or HBM/advanced-packaging order push-out) that isn't in the news feed provided.
research_pointers: Check for VECO pre-announcement, analyst downgrade, or customer (ASML/Samsung/SK Hynix) order commentary today
Verify no 8-K guide-down was filed - none shown in feed but move size implies company-specific catalyst
Cross-reference with ONTO and AEIS also down double-digits - suggests semi-cap group event, not AI narrative

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Onto's metrology/inspection tools are levered to advanced packaging (HBM, CoWoS) - core AI infrastructure. If Zuck comment were the driver, we'd expect NVDA/AMD weakness too. Coincident -12% with VECO -18% and AEIS -12% strongly suggests a semi-cap sector catalyst (possibly China export controls, TSMC capex cut rumor, or peer guide-down) not captured in provided news.
research_pointers: Look for semi-cap sector catalyst today (ASML/AMAT/LRCX moves, China policy)
Advanced packaging demand signals from TSMC July commentary
This is likely NOT the Zuck-AI-agent story

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Advanced Energy sells power delivery for semi tools + data center power. AI capex slowdown narrative could hit, but AEIS is also a data-center power play which benefits from AI. -12% on volume 0.97x average is odd - low volume suggests thin liquidity move or specific news I don't have.
research_pointers: Check AEIS for guide-down or analyst action
Semi-cap cluster (VECO/ONTO/AEIS all down big same day) - find the shared catalyst

ai_analysis.no_signals_note: The provided AI news (Zuck AI-agents comment, browser wars piece) is too soft to explain the double-digit semi-cap drawdowns in VECO/ONTO/AEIS. The clustered move strongly implies a sector-specific catalyst (peer guide-down, export controls, or hyperscaler capex signal) not present in the news feed. Recommend Michael source the actual catalyst before treating any of these as AI-narrative overreactions. AVAV/KTOS strength is defense/drone, unrelated to provided AI headlines. CRSP, PTCT, DECK, FIZZ moves are non-AI (biotech/consumer/earnings).
